frontmatter.title: Introduction to the GDEX THREDDS Data Server

affiliations.name: NSF National Center for Atmospheric Research

children.value: provides remote access to datasets via OPeNDAP, NCSS, WMS, and other standard protocols — without downloading files. This notebook introduces TDS concepts and shows how to navigate the catalog.
Identify available data services for a dataset
Accessing TDS via the Web Browser

children.label: Accessing TDS via the Web Browser

children.value: The GDEX TDS catalog can be explored interactively through any web browser — no Python or programming required. This section walks through the key steps.
Step 1: Open the GDEX TDS Catalog

children.label: Step 1: Open the GDEX TDS Catalog

children.value: Navigate to the GDEX TDS catalog root in your browser:
You will see a list of top-level dataset collections. Each entry corresponds to a GDEX dataset identified by its dataset ID (e.g.,

children.alt: GDEX TDS catalog root page showing dataset collections

children.value: GDEX TDS catalog root — list of available dataset collections.
) to enter its sub-catalog. Sub-catalogs are organized by year or other time partitions. Click through until you reach a directory containing individual files.

children.alt: Click through until you see individual files

children.value: Step 3: View Available Services for a File

children.label: Step 3: View Available Services for a File

children.value: Click on an individual file name to open its dataset page. The page lists all access services (OPeNDAP, NCSS, WMS, HTTP download, etc.) with direct links — you can copy any URL for use in Python or paste it into a browser to download.

children.alt: Dataset detail page showing available access services

children.value: Dataset detail page — all available service URLs are listed here.
Accessing TDS via Siphon — Programmatic Approach

children.label: Accessing TDS via Siphon — Programmatic Approach

children.value: is a Python client for THREDDS and other remote data services. It can:
inspect available datasets and access URLs
work with remote data through OPeNDAP, NCSS, and related services
for analysis of remote NetCDF data
Use it when you want to discover and access datasets without downloading full files first.

from siphon.catalog import TDSCatalog
import xarray as xr
The GDEX TDS catalog root URL is
. You can navigate sub-catalogs by dataset ID (dnnnnnn where n are digits) . Here, we demostrate a dataset d010049 : Global Ocean Physics Reanalysis as a example below.

# open the GDEX TDS catalog
cat = TDSCatalog("https://tds.gdex.ucar.edu/thredds/catalog/files/d010049/1993/catalog.xml")
print(cat.datasets)
TDS provides multiple access protocols for each dataset. Common ones include:
— remote array subsetting, works with xarray

# pring out one of the datasets included in the catalog
list(cat.datasets.values())[0]

# inspect services available for a dataset
ds = list(cat.datasets.values())[0] # we are getting the first dataset in the catalog

print(f'Services available for this dataset ({ds.name}):')
for service_name, service_url in ds.access_urls.items():
    print(f'- {service_name}: {service_url}')
# print(ds.access_urls)

- JupyterNotebook: https://tds.gdex.ucar.edu/thredds/notebook/files/d010049/1993/mercatorglorys12v1_gl12_mean_19930101_R19930106.nc

children.value: exposes multiple access protocols through the GDEX THREDDS Data Server.
— Remote data access and subsetting over HTTP; commonly used with tools like
for analysis without downloading full files.
— NetCDF Subset Service (NCSS) for requesting specific variables, time ranges, vertical levels, and geographic regions.
— Binary remote access protocol for CDM-compatible clients; useful for efficient programmatic data retrieval.
— Feature-oriented CDM remote access for structured scientific data types (for example, grids and point features).
— Launch/open notebook-based workflows connected to the dataset in an interactive environment.
— Web Coverage Service for retrieving geospatial raster/coverage data with spatial and dimensional subsetting.
— Web Map Service for requesting rendered map images of dataset layers for visualization.
— Direct file download access over HTTP.
— ISO-standard metadata representation for cataloging and discovery.
— NetCDF Markup Language view describing dataset structure and metadata in XML form.
— Unified Data Discovery Convention metadata endpoint for interoperable dataset discovery.
These services support different workflows, from quick map visualization (
The GDEX THREDDS Data Server exposes datasets through standard remote-access protocols.
